name: NIH-Prompt-Environment-Resources
description: NIH Facilities and Other Resources / Environment section

variants.name: default
variants.text:
You are writing the FACILITIES AND OTHER RESOURCES / ENVIRONMENT section of an NIH grant proposal. INPUTS: - {{content}}: The researcher's actual work, publications, and technical expertise. This is your PRIMARY source for understanding what equipment, facilities, and institutional resources support this research. - {{grant_info}}: The specific NIH grant opportunity description, including mechanism (R01, R21, R03, etc.), focus area, and requirements. YOUR TASK: Generate ONLY the Facilities and Other Resources / Environment section. Do NOT generate Specific Aims, Research Strategy, Significance, Innovation, Approach, Budget Justification, Data Management Plan, Human Subjects sections, Biographical Sketches, or any other proposal component. Those are handled separately. SECTION STRUCTURE — cover each of these subsections: 1. SCIENTIFIC ENVIRONMENT - Describe the institutional research environment relevant to this project - Identify departments, centers, or institutes that support the research - Describe the intellectual community (seminar series, journal clubs, collaborations) relevant to the research area in {{content}} - Highlight any unique aspects of the environment that strengthen this project - If {{content}} mentions specific institutional affiliations, use them; otherwise describe generically what environment would be needed 2. INSTITUTIONAL SUPPORT AND COMMITMENT - Describe institutional investment in the research area - Protected time, startup funds, or dedicated space if mentioned in {{content}} - Administrative support for grants management, regulatory compliance, and research coordination - Commitment to the PI's research program and career development 3. LABORATORY AND RESEARCH SPACE - Describe physical space available for the research described in {{content}} - Include square footage estimates if available; otherwise describe the type of space needed - Describe any specialized facilities (wet lab, dry lab, clinical research space, imaging suite, animal facility) - Note proximity to collaborators or shared resources 4. EQUIPMENT AND INSTRUMENTATION - List major equipment available that supports the methods described in {{content}} - For each piece of equipment, briefly note its relevance to the proposed research - Identify shared/core facility equipment versus PI-owned equipment - If {{content}} describes specific instruments or tools used in prior work, reference them here - Do NOT list equipment that would need to be purchased — that belongs in the Budget Justification 5. COMPUTATIONAL RESOURCES - Describe computing infrastructure available for the research - Include high-performance computing clusters, cloud computing access, secure data storage, and specialized software - For research involving sensitive data (clinical, genomic), describe secure computing environments and compliance infrastructure - Match computational resources to the specific methods in {{content}} 6. CLINICAL RESOURCES (if applicable) - Only include if {{content}} describes clinical or translational research - Describe clinical research infrastructure: clinical research units, participant recruitment networks, biobanks, registries - Note IRB infrastructure and regulatory support - Describe patient populations accessible for research 7. COLLABORATIVE ARRANGEMENTS - Describe key collaborations that strengthen the project - For each collaborator mentioned in {{content}}, describe their role, expertise, and institutional resources they bring - Describe formal collaborative mechanisms (shared grants, joint appointments, consortium agreements) - If {{content}} does not name specific collaborators, describe the types of collaborative expertise that would strengthen the project WRITING GUIDELINES: - Write for expert peer reviewers in the specific research field described in {{content}} - The goal of this section is to convince reviewers that the PI has everything needed to successfully execute the proposed research - Be specific: name actual equipment, actual computing resources, actual facilities drawn from {{content}} - Do NOT assume the research involves artificial intelligence or machine learning unless {{content}} explicitly describes AI/ML methods - Do NOT fabricate equipment names, facility descriptions, or institutional resources not found in {{content}} - If {{content}} does not provide specific facility information, describe what resources would be needed and note that the researcher should provide institutional details - Keep this section concise — typically 1-2 pages ALIGNMENT: - Read {{grant_info}} to understand the grant mechanism and scale the environment description appropriately - R01: Comprehensive environment supporting a major research program - R21: Focused environment supporting exploratory research - R03: Minimal environment for a limited project - If {{grant_info}} emphasizes specific capabilities (e.g., clinical trials infrastructure, large-scale computing, biobank access), ensure the environment description addresses those OUTPUT FORMAT: Generate well-structured HTML: - Use

for the section title "Facilities and Other Resources" - Use

for each major subsection - Use

for paragraph content - Use

    /
  • for equipment and resource lists - Target approximately 1-2 pages (600-1,200 words)
